candidates.why: Direct balance-sheet cash line matching cash and cash equivalents.

variables.notes: Preferred balance sheet cash line exists.

candidates.why: Direct match for total current liabilities on the balance sheet.
Balancing line, not the reported total current liabilities.

variables.notes: Clear reported total current liabilities line.

candidates.why: Clean current financing debt line on the balance sheet; preferred over maturity component lines because it appears to represent the reported current debt total excluding lease liabilities.
Current portion of long-term debt and a valid STD component, but likely narrower than the cleaner total current debt line.
Financial liabilities could include debt-like items, but label is too broad and not a clean debt line.

variables.notes: Chose a non-shortlist label because it is the cleanest reported short-term debt line. Excluded lease liabilities per instructions and avoided double counting with 'Debt - Long-Term - Maturities - within 1 Year'.

candidates.why: Direct match for taxes payable/current tax liabilities.
Largest reported net tangible operating asset class and closest balance-sheet analogue to net PPE, but it may exclude equipment and projects.
A net tangible fixed asset component, but clearly not total PPE.
Tangible asset component under construction, but not total PPE.
Tangible right-of-use asset line, but baseline PPE usually prefers owned PPE and not leases as the main PPE proxy.

variables.notes: No clean total property, plant and equipment line is reported. 'Vessels' appears to be the main net PPE line, but it may understate total PPE because equipment and newbuildings/projects are shown separately.

candidates.why: Direct match for total assets on the balance sheet.

candidates.why: Direct match for net cash flow from operating activities.
